wheelwright.tagline: We aren't reinventing the wheel - we're evolving it faster than one person ever could.

_wai_bootstrap._purpose: Instructions for AI assistants to find or create Wheelwright components

check_order: 2. Check if 'WAI' command is in PATH
5. Search for directory containing 'WAI' CLI and 'teach.py'
find_framework.if_not_found: Ask user: 'Where is your Wheelwright framework installed? (git clone https://github.com/wheelwright-ai/framework)'

check_order: 4. Check framework's connected_wheels for this project

update_paths.after_discovery: Update framework_path and hub_path in wheelwright

identity.name: Tracks
identity.one_liner: Portable conversation records for any AI session. Take your context anywhere.
identity.success_looks_like: Anyone can generate a WAI Track file from any AI conversation using the prompts in this repo. Samples are realistic enough to demonstrate portability. Wheelwright is the clear next step.

in_scope: Three prompt variants: closing-request, prep-and-request, active-collection
Three realistic sample Track files demonstrating portability
README as the manifesto: portability framing, use cases, Wheelwright funnel
out_of_scope: Application code, web interface, build system
Track file viewer or editor (that may be a future spoke)
constraints: Pure documentation — no package.json, no node_modules, no build step
All wheelwright.ai and framework repo links must be present
Prompts must be self-contained (raw URL usable as LLM instruction)
Samples must be realistic enough to demonstrate portability

approach.workflow: Public GitHub repo with a spec, prompts, and samples that prove portable track generation while pointing users toward Wheelwright for native automation
approach.review_process: Mario reviews content quality and messaging

philosophy.core_principle: AI as responsible partner, not just enabler
behaviors: Detect scope drift and flag before enabling
Require explicit acknowledgment for direction changes
Prefer 'are you sure?' over silent compliance

evolution_log.change: Foundation completed via inception lug trk-inception-v2
evolution_log.rationale: Self-inception file authored by Sparky (PathFinder), enriched by framework, delivered to Tracks inbox

evolution_log.change: Reconciled stale dashboard/app metadata back to the shipped documentation-and-prompts repo
evolution_log.rationale: README, spec, prompts, and samples are the source of truth; legacy build-teaching residue had drifted state and protocol files away from what this repository actually is

evolution_log.change: Started folder-based Skills migration with wakeup as the first authoritative skill
evolution_log.rationale: Commands should live inside discoverable skill folders with explicit exposure, routing, and inheritance instead of accumulating in a flat command directory

evolution_log.change: Completed the spoke-level Skills cutover and demoted legacy command docs to compatibility surfaces
evolution_log.rationale: `Skills/` is now the intended authoring and routing architecture for wakeup, core utilities, and advisors; remaining work is reconciliation, policy, and cleanup around transitional mirrors

evolution_log.change: Settled the long-term Skills authority policy
evolution_log.rationale: The repo now has a concrete ownership model: `Skills/index.jsonl` is the routing authority, skill folders own behavior, `WAI-Spoke/WAI-Skills.jsonl` is mirror-only, and legacy `wai-*` files are compatibility aliases during the transition

evolution_log.change: Re-ranked the remaining hub teaching queue for the Skills architecture
evolution_log.rationale: Post-cutover work now has an explicit order: reliability and teaching-ingestion changes come first, `skill-system-v1` must translate into the settled folder-based model, and malformed or conflicting closeout metadata stays blocked until adjudicated

feature_toggles._description: Toggle features for baseline comparison and testing

wheel.name: Tracks
wheel.description: Portable conversation records and prompt library for WAI Track generation

next_actions: Keep guide, README, and state language aligned to the settled authority model while cleanup stays conservative
Resolve metadata-missing teachings so each remaining hub teaching has enough target and ownership data to map cleanly into Skills/ or another clearly non-authoritative surface
Resolve the wai-closeout-step9b-sender-v1 conflict against Skills/closeout before any closeout-specific teaching adoption
Review track-reliability-v1 first, then wai-step3a-path-split-v1 and track-chain-protocol-v1, as the highest-priority post-cutover teaching sequence
Translate skill-system-v1 into the settled folder-based Skills model without reopening the authority policy or restoring flat-command ownership
Triage test-pipeline-verify-v1, shipit-makefile-quality-gate-v1, and wai-shipit-release-tag-v1 against the repo's docs-only constraints before adopting any delivery or release automation
Implement generated or mechanically synchronized upkeep for WAI-Spoke/WAI-Skills.jsonl without changing its mirror-only role
Run final verification on guide, state, router, mirror, and compatibility alias coherence
Add GitHub repo topics for discoverability
Link Tracks from wheelwright.ai and the framework README
blockers: WAI-Spoke/WAI-Skills.jsonl remains a transitional mirror until generated or mechanically synchronized maintenance is implemented
Legacy wai command alias lifetime and retirement criteria still need an explicit cleanup plan
Advisor inheritance depth and whether skills may expose more than one entry document remain open design questions
Final verification has not yet confirmed whether any active caller still depends on specific legacy wai compatibility aliases
Metadata-missing and malformed hub teachings still need normalization before they can be mapped safely to Skills/ paths or compatibility surfaces
wai-closeout-step9b-sender-v1 remains an unresolved conflict against the current Skills/closeout sender and sequencing model
wai-step3a-path-split-v1 overlaps the existing Step 3a reconciliation signal and must be adjudicated to avoid duplicate teaching-ingestion behavior
Shipit and release-oriented teachings assume delivery surfaces this documentation-and-prompts repo does not currently own
insights: Tracks is a documentation and prompt library — no app code, no build system
Three prompt variants (closing-request, prep-and-request, active-collection) are the core deliverable
Portability framing: frees intellectual product trapped in the LLM chat
WAI-Spoke state should defer to README/spec/prompts/samples when hub teachings or session metadata drift
Rich v2 prompt variants materially improve continuity by capturing user voice, pivotal statements, reconciled artifacts, and explicit next-agent handoff notes
The settled authority model is: `Skills/index.jsonl` owns routing, skill folders own behavior, `WAI-Spoke/WAI-Skills.jsonl` is mirror-only, and legacy command files are compatibility aliases
Guide, README, and state docs now describe the same settled authority model and keep cleanup conservative by leaving compatibility aliases in place
As long as WAI-Spoke/WAI-Skills.jsonl remains a mirrored artifact, it is a drift risk unless generation or mechanical synchronization replaces manual upkeep
Advisor behavior is now defined by a shared parent plus child overrides, so policy clarity matters more than adding more transitional prose in legacy command files

The remaining hub teaching queue is no longer a flat backlog: reliability and teaching-ingestion changes rank ahead of skill-system refinements, while malformed closeout or metadata teachings stay blocked until clarified

ai_rules.context_loading: Read WAI-Spoke/WAI-Guide.md first, then WAI-State.json and WAI-State.md

foundation_enforcement.on_incomplete: CRITICAL: Guide user through foundation questions before any work. Do not skip.
foundation_enforcement.on_drift_detected: Present options, require explicit choice before proceeding
foundation_enforcement.on_evolution: Log change with rationale and acknowledgment in evolution_log

stewardship.principle: AI is the responsible partner, not just an enabler
behaviors: Detect scope drift and flag before enabling
Require explicit acknowledgment for direction changes
Prefer 'are you sure?' over silent compliance

signals.instruction: Append to WAI-Signals.jsonl if decision impact >= 8

wai_commands.time: Intelligent token usage estimate with capacity warnings
wai_commands.rules: List active rules and behavioral guidelines
wai_commands.closeout: Provide updated WAI-State files for session end

environments._note: Registry of environments that have worked on this project. Auto-populated by closeout.

model_log._note: Track model switches within and across sessions. Enables retrospective analysis of model-task fit.

Station Schema — lines, lineEvents, extend sessions + trackArtifacts
Epic — Station Master + Lines: Track Assembly & Continuity Layer
